scope.summary: Implement a delete_seam authoring operation that removes a SeamSpec entry from a draft GarmentSpec via a CRDT tombstone, emits a TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded event, and allows the garment to be re-simmed with the panels decoupled
acceptance_criteria: The edit_seam MCP tool (or a dedicated delete_seam variant) accepts (garment_id, seam_id) and applies a seam tombstone per GAR-CRDT-002, emitting TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded
The next sandbox simulation run after deletion treats the previously joined edges as open (no seam constraint between them)
Deleting a seam that is referenced by a trim tack attachment produces a SEAM_EDGE_REF advisory warning (not blocking) listing the affected trim_ids
The SEAM_EDGE_REF fast-check is re-run after deletion to confirm the remaining SeamSpecs are self-consistent
Concurrent delete and add of the same seam_id resolves via set-union (add wins over delete for same seam_id in the same CRDT clock) consistent with GAR-CRDT-002
scope.risk_if_missed: Seam deletion is essential for iterative garment construction — a model may author a garment, discover a redundant or incorrect seam from the SimulationReceipt, and need to remove it before re-simming. Without delete_seam the only path is to re-author the entire GarmentSpec from scratch, which breaks the self-correction loop and makes the model-first authoring pipeline brittle.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
